name: Usategieta / Las palomeras – circular from Lizarrieta to Col de Nabarlatz
name_original: Usategieta / Las palomeras – circular desde Lizarrieta hasta Col de Nabarlatz
region_intro: Hiking around Etxalar offers diverse landscapes within the Pyrenean foothills, characterized by varying elevations and dense woodlands. The region features rolling hills and lush forests, with the River Bidasoa contributing to scenic riverside paths. This area provides a range of hiking experiences, from gentle walks to more challenging mountain trails. Best hiking trails around Etxalar The most popular hiking route is Usategieta / Las palomeras – circular from Lizarrieta to Col de Nabarlatz, a 3.3 miles (5.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 33 minutes to complete. This easy route offers views of the surrounding hills and forests. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ibanteli – Ibanteli 698 m loop from Lizarrietako lepoa, a moderate 6.3 miles (10.1 km) path. This trail ascends to the summit of Ibanteli, providing panoramic views of the Pyrenean landscape. Local hikers also love the Kokori (723 m) – Mendixorrotx (596 m) loop from Etxalar, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail leading through varied forest and open terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 53 minutes. Hiking around Etxalar is defined by Pyrenean foothills, dense woodlands, and scenic riverside paths. The extensive network of routes offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more demanding mountain ascents. The routes in Etxalar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 1900 hikers have used komoot to explore Etxalar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Etxalar?
A: There are over 25 hiking trails around Etxalar, offering a range of experiences from easy walks to more challenging mountain ascents. The extensive network of routes caters to different ability levels, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Etxalar?
A: Yes, Etxalar offers several easy routes. For a gentle introduction, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970344075/usategieta-las-palomeras-circular-from-lizarrieta-to-col-de-nabarlatz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Usategieta / Las palomeras – circular from Lizarrieta to Col de Nabarlatz&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.3-mile (5.3 km) trail. The region also features the Bidasoa greenway, a nearly level 40 km path along a former train track, perfect for leisurely walks.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I see while hiking in Etxalar?
A: Etxalar's trails lead through diverse landscapes including Pyrenean foothills, dense woodlands, and scenic riverside paths along the River Bidasoa. You can explore natural features like the Infernuko Errota (Mill of Hell) and the Xorroxin Waterfall in nearby Erratzu. For panoramic views, routes ascend to summits like Ibanteli and La Rhune.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for those seeking a challenge, Etxalar provides more demanding treks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20520071" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of La Rhune's summit – La Rhune Summit loop from Lizarrietako lepoa&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 11.8-mile (19 km) route with significant elevation gain, offering extensive views from the summit. Sections of the long-distance GR 11 also pass through the area, providing options for longer, more strenuous hikes.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Etxalar?
A: Many of Etxalar's routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10517471" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibanteli – Ibanteli 698 m loop from Lizarrietako lepoa&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 6.3-mile (10.1 km) path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10517445" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Kokori (723 m) – Mendixorrotx (596 m) loop from Etxalar&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.9 miles (11.1 km) long.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Etxalar?
A: The Pyrenean foothills around Etxalar offer pleasant hiking conditions during spring, summer, and early autumn. These seasons provide milder temperatures and lush scenery. While winter hiking is possible, some higher routes may require appropriate gear due to snow or ice.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Etxalar?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 300 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, from rolling hills and dense woodlands to scenic riverside paths, and the panoramic views from higher elevations.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, beyond the natural beauty, you can find interesting landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1016283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Rhune summit&lt;/a&gt; offers incredible vistas. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/852512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Ignace Pass&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/732868" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Notre-Dame-d'Aubépine chapel&lt;/a&gt;. The nearby Señorío de Bertiz Nature Park is also a significant attraction for nature lovers.

Q: Is public transport available to access hiking trailheads in Etxalar?
A: While specific public transport links directly to all trailheads might be limited, Etxalar is generally accessible. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for connections to the village, from which many routes originate or are within walking distance. For more remote starting points, a car might be necessary.

Q: Are there options for long-distance hiking in the Etxalar area?
A: Etxalar is a point along the GR 11, also known as the Senda Pirenaica, a significant long-distance route traversing the Spanish Pyrenees. Sections of the GR 11 around Etxalar, such as the path from Col de Lizarrieta to Elizondo, offer opportunities for multi-day treks with easy, wide paths and secluded views.

Q: Can I find places to stay or eat near the hiking routes in Etxalar?
A: Etxalar itself offers various amenities including accommodation and dining options. Along some routes, you might find huts or shelters like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3135125" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gîte Trapero Baita&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6821605" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benta Beltza Shelter&lt;/a&gt;, which can be useful for longer hikes. It's always a good idea to plan your stops in advance.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails around Etxalar?
A: The diverse landscapes of Etxalar, including dense woodlands and river valleys, support a variety of wildlife. Hikers might spot various bird species, small mammals, and local fauna characteristic of the Pyrenean foothills. Respecting their natural habitat and maintaining a quiet presence increases your chances of an encounter.
tips: Small shop with snacks and drinks. I don't know the exact opening times. But you shouldn't come too late.
Section of the GR 11 Long Distance trail that runs along the border between Spain and France with impressive views of the entire Pyrenees chain.
Wherever you look, beauty in its purest form in this section of the Senda Pyrenees very close to the border with France.
